NLP.trend_synthesis: Recent NLP research clusters around three themes: efficient fine-tuning of large language models for domain adaptation, evaluation frameworks for LLM robustness on low-resource languages, and multimodal reasoning combining text with visual or structured inputs. The field is moving toward smaller but specialized models rather than scaling parameter counts.

papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
On the Robustness of Instruction-Tuned Models
Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
Multimodal Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
A Survey of Hallucination in Large Language Models
Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive Tasks
Fine-tuning Language Models for Low-Resource Languages
Text-to-SQL with Large Language Models: A Survey

Computer Vision.trend_synthesis: Computer vision research is dominated by foundational vision transformers, self-supervised pretraining strategies, and domain adaptation from general to specialized (medical, satellite) imagery. Diffusion models have opened new frontiers in image generation. Efficient architectures for edge deployment and video understanding also feature prominently.

papers.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition
Segment Anything
Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
Domain-Adversarial Training of Neural Networks
Attention U-Net: Learning Where to Look for the Pancreas

Cybersecurity.trend_synthesis: The cybersecurity landscape is shifting toward AI-powered threats and defenses. Key themes include adversarial robustness of ML models, backdoor attacks on federated learning, differential privacy guarantees, and post-quantum cryptography readiness. LLM-powered social engineering and LLM-assisted vulnerability detection are emerging frontiers.

papers.title: Intriguing Properties of Neural Networks
Federated Learning: Strategies for Improving Communication Efficiency
BadNets: Identifying Vulnerabilities in the Machine Learning Model Supply Chain
Deep Learning with Differential Privacy
Post-Quantum Cryptography: Current State and Future Directions

IoT.trend_synthesis: IoT research is converging with edge AI, with a focus on running inference on resource-constrained devices via TinyML and model quantization. Energy harvesting for battery-free sensors, LoRaWAN-based mesh networking, and federated learning at the edge are dominant themes.

papers.title: TinyML: Machine Learning for Embedded Systems
LoRa: A Low-Power Wide-Area Network Technology
Federated Learning on Edge Devices: A Survey
Energy Harvesting for IoT: A Comprehensive Survey

Cloud Computing.trend_synthesis: Cloud research focuses on serverless computing patterns, carbon-aware workload scheduling, chaos engineering for microservice resilience, and multi-cloud orchestration. Kubernetes-native architectures dominate, with growing interest in eBPF-based observability and WebAssembly at the edge.

papers.title: Kubernetes: Up and Running
Serverless Computing: One Step Forward, Two Steps Back
Chaos Engineering: Principles and Practice
Carbon-Aware Data Center Operations

Reinforcement Learning.trend_synthesis: RL research is pivoting toward real-world deployment: safe RL with formal verification, multi-agent coordination for warehouse/logistics automation, and offline RL from static datasets. Model-based approaches are gaining ground over model-free for sample efficiency, and RL-from-human-feedback (RLHF) has become critical for LLM alignment.

papers.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
Mastering the Game of Go with Deep Neural Networks
Safe Reinforcement Learning: A Survey
Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning: A Survey
Training Language Models to Follow Instructions

5G.trend_synthesis: 5G and beyond research centers on massive MIMO channel estimation, deep unfolding for signal processing, URLLC schemes for industrial IoT, and network slicing orchestration. AI-native 6G designs and integrated sensing-communication are emerging themes.

papers.title: Massive MIMO Networks: Spectral, Energy, and Hardware Efficiency
Deep Unfolding for Communication Systems
Network Slicing in 5G: A Survey
URLLC in Industrial IoT: Challenges and Solutions

AI_general.trend_synthesis: Broad AI research themes include: foundational model architectures beyond transformers (state-space models, mixture-of-experts), AI governance and fairness audits, watermarking generative content, agentic workflows with tool use, and scaling laws for data-constrained regimes.

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
On the Opportunities and Risks of Foundation Models
Watermarking Generative AI Outputs
Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling
